page: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The server is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 56.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 459 GB drive, and there are no onboard GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and processing speed. The CPU performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks falling into the bottom 10%. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are categorized as weak. However, memory subsystems perform better, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 336.87 GB/sec, though large-block write bandwidth drops to a poor 14.26 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also register in the weak tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS4-4 offers a massive memory footprint relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized option where RAM capacity is the primary constraint rather than raw CPU throughput. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute, such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy data processing tasks with low CPU demands.
description: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and a substantial 224.0 GB of memory, yielding a high ratio of 56.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 459 GB of local storage and no GPUs, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show a significant tradeoff: while memory capacity is massive, CPU performance is limited, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the weak and poor categories. Memory bandwidth for small blocks is average at 336.87 GB/sec, but large-block write bandwidth is poor. Consequently, the GS4-4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound tasks rather than compute-heavy operations. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing workloads with low CPU requirements.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine delivering a massive 224 GB memory footprint and local storage for memory-constrained, low-compute workloads.
